title: Maps, Strings, Bytes, Runes, Unicode, and Deterministic Results · Guided Systems Studio
summary: An incident summarizer must count labels, normalize text, preserve UTF-8, and publish stable output despite unordered maps. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Unicode-safe deterministic incident summarizer while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, update, delete, clear, and query maps while distinguishing missing keys from stored zero values.
Choose comparable key types and explain map reference behavior, iteration instability, mutation rules, and concurrency limits.
Treat strings as immutable byte sequences and convert to or from byte slices with explicit allocation and encoding intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Map Create Access
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Incident labels include "café", "café", "東京", invalid UTF-8, and 4 repeated ASCII keys. Count without confusing missing and zero, report byte and rune evidence, then sort keys for deterministic output.
